channel.title: The Brussels Times
channel.description: Latest posts from The Brussels Times.

item.title: Dutch Gaza 'flotilla' participants file sexual violence complaint against Israeli army
item.description: In the complaint, they allege sexual violence, assault, humiliation and torture at the hands of Israeli soldiers after being arrested in international waters.

item.title: OpenAI proposes a 5% stake to the US Government
item.description: CEO of the company behind ChatGPT, Sam Altman, reportedly favours extending such arrangements to all leading AI developers in the United States.

item.title: Uncage EU legislation: MEPs protest against caging of farmed animals
item.description: “Millions of hens, pigs and other animals in the EU suffer in extreme confinement every single day."

item.title: Canada will participate in Eurovision Song Contest next year
item.description: It is the first new country to join since Australia in 2015.

item.title: Sea surface temperatures break records
item.description: Exceptionally high global sea surface temperatures will have an impact on marine ecosystems, weather patterns and the climate across the entire planet.

item.title: Euroclear sues Russian Central Bank in Brussels court
item.description: Euroclear holds around €200 billion of Russian funds, which have been blocked due to sanctions imposed by the EU following the invasion of Ukraine.

item.title: Belgium sends shelter supplies and experts to earthquake-hit Venezuela
item.description: The earthquakes, measuring 7.2 and 7.5 on the Richter scale, struck Venezuela on 24 June.

item.title: Israel’s aid restrictions force people in Gaza to endure summer heat in tents
item.description: “It is an outrage that families in Gaza, after months of displacement and loss, now face summer heat in makeshift tents."

item.title: Pope urges world to 'resolve conflicts as human beings, not as beasts'
item.description: In his homily, Leo XIV lamented that "international tensions and conflicts continue to gravely wound the human family".

item.title: Belgium 'ready to help' as dozens die and hundreds injured in Venezuela earthquake
item.description: At least 32 people have died and 700 were injured following two earthquakes and aftershocks that shook Venezuela on Wednesday.

item.title: Trump tells Rutte that NATO countries have 'let down' the United States
item.description: "Give us a little help, give us a little kiss," said US President Donald Trump during a meeting with NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte at the White House.

item.title: North Korea's warships to be equipped with nuclear weapons, says Kim Jong Un
item.description: North Korea's Kim Jong Un announced plans to equip the navy with nuclear weapons and build 10,000-tonne warships.

item.title: Number of dangerously humid hot days has more than doubled in 50 years
item.description: The global average of humid hot days has risen to 23 per year – 19 of which are linked to human-induced climate change.

item.title: First case of Ebola confirmed in France
item.description: The Ebola outbreak in the DRC and Uganda has so far infected more than 1,000 people ​and ​killed ⁠267 – the highest number of ​confirmed cases within ​the ⁠first month of any episode of the disease.

item.title: AI could bypass cybersecurity measures 'in a matter of months', say spy agencies
item.description: The Five Eyes intelligence alliance issues a rare warning urging governments to act swiftly to adapt to the challenges posed by rapid advancements in AI.

item.title: More tankers are passing through Strait of Hormuz amid fragile ceasefire
item.description: Israel has said it's continuing operations in Lebanon despite the Iranian demand that fighting there stop to reach a regional peace agreement.

item.title: Japanese Emperor treated to official state ceremony in Brussels
item.description: The state visit of the Japanese imperial couple officially started on Tuesday morning with a welcome ceremony at the Royal Palace in Brussels.

item.title: Amnesty condemns Libya's migrant crackdown and growing ties with EU
item.description: Systematic human rights violations are being allegedly committed against refugees and migrants in Libya, perpetuated with the active support of EU Member States.

item.title: Judge overturns Trump administration's subpoenas against Minnesota
item.description: A US federal judge on Monday dismissed subpoenas issued by the Trump administration against authorities of the state of Minnesota.

item.title: US-Iran talks kicks-off in Switzerland with EU sidelined
item.description: "The MoU provides an opportunity for regional stability and the full restoration of freedom of navigation and safe transit through the Strait of Hormuz."
